steps.title: Recall the vertical shift rule
steps.content:
To shift a graph up by $k$ units, add $k$ to the output of the function:
$$g(x)=f(x)+k$$

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — $g(x)=4x^{6}+7$: $g(x)=4x^6+7$: adds 7 to the output — a vertical shift up by 7. ✓
  • Choice B — $g(x)=4x^{6}-7$: $g(x)=4x^6-7$: subtracts 7, which shifts down by 7. Eliminate.
  • Choice C — $g(x)=4x^{42}$: $g(x)=4x^{42}$: changes the exponent — not a shift at all. Eliminate.
  • Choice D — $g(x)=28x^{6}$: $g(x)=28x^6$: a vertical stretch, not a shift. Eliminate.
That is the correct answer.
